---
title: "Tokenize text into word events"
subtitle: "A base extractor that returns an `EventSeries`."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You have transcript text and need word-like events for language or alignment workflows.

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows import extract_features

result = extract_features(
    "The scene opens on a quiet room.",
    features=["text.tokenize"],
)
words = result.features["text.tokenize"]

list(zip(words.label.tolist(), words.onset_s.tolist(), words.offset_s.tolist()))
```

## Why the result includes word intervals

The result has one onset and one offset for every token, even when the source
text did not include observed timings. It is therefore an `EventSeries`, the
same object type used for words returned by speech recognition or OCR. Code
that works with event labels and intervals can process all three sources in
the same way.

## Related feature IDs

- `text.tokenize`
- `language.surface`
- `speech.words`
- `image.ocr`
- `video.ocr`
